---
title: "allison gng"
output: html_document
date: "2026-05-29"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# ============================================================
# Go/NoGo Task Preprocessing Pipeline
# ============================================================
# Description:
#   1. Audits raw CSV files for usable trial data
#   2. Extracts behavioral metrics per subject (accuracy, RT, d-prime)
#   3. Generates BIDS-format events.tsv files per subject per run
#   4. Generates FSL-format 3-column regressor .txt files for first-level fMRI
#
# Regressor structure (13 EVs):
#   - low_hit, high_hit          : correct go responses by stake
#   - low_miss, high_miss        : missed go trials by stake
#   - low_CR, high_CR            : correct nogo (successful inhibition) by stake
#   - low_FA, high_FA            : false alarms (failed inhibition) by stake
#   - low_cue, high_cue          : block cue onset (nuisance)
#   - reward                     : reward feedback onset (nuisance, 6/run)
#   - late_response              : responses after 500ms target window (nuisance)
#
# Contrast matrix:
#   Successful inhibition > Go : low_CR=1, high_CR=1, low_hit=-1, high_hit=-1
#   Failed inhibition > Go     : low_FA=1, high_FA=1, low_hit=-1, high_hit=-1
#
# Author: Allison Langer
# Date:   3/12/2026
# ============================================================

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(tidyverse)

# ============================================================
# CONFIGURATION — update paths here
# ============================================================

root_dir    <- "/Users/andrewcardona/Desktop/gng/data"
events_dir  <- file.path(root_dir, "events_tsv")
output_dir  <- events_dir   # FSL .txt files written alongside TSVs

beh_output   <- "/Users/andrewcardona/Desktop/gng/gng_behavioral_results.csv"
audit_output <- "/Users/andrewcardona/Desktop/gng/trial_counts_audit.csv"

# Minimum trials per regressor per run to flag as sparse
sparse_threshold <- 5


# Late response rate thresholds by age group (proportion, not percent)
late_resp_threshold_children    <- 0.28   # 1000s — children
late_resp_threshold_adolescents <- 0.10   # 2000s — adolescents
late_resp_threshold_adults      <- 0.10   # 3000s — adults
# ============================================================
# COMBINE ONLY TRUE CRASH/RESUME FILES BY PID + LITe WAVE
# ============================================================
# If a PID/wave has only one file, it is NOT combined.
# If a PID/wave has multiple files and at least one filename
# contains resume-round, those files are combined.
# ============================================================

combined_dir <- file.path(root_dir, "combined_by_pid_wave")
dir.create(combined_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)

extract_pid <- function(filename) {
  pid <- str_match(filename, "(?i)_P(\\d{3,4})")
  as.numeric(pid[, 2])
}

extract_wave <- function(filename) {
  wave <- str_match(filename, "(?i)LITe\\.(\\d+)")
  as.numeric(wave[, 2])
}

is_resume_file <- function(filename) {
  str_detect(
    filename,
    regex("RESUME.*ROUND.*\\d+", ignore_case = TRUE)
  )
}
raw_file_list <- list.files(
  path = root_dir,
  pattern = "\\.csv$",
  recursive = TRUE,
  full.names = TRUE
)

# Do not re-read files created by this combining step
raw_file_list <- raw_file_list[!str_detect(raw_file_list, "combined_by_pid_wave")]

file_key <- tibble(
  file = raw_file_list,
  filename = basename(raw_file_list),
  pid = extract_pid(basename(raw_file_list)),
  wave = extract_wave(basename(raw_file_list)),
  is_resume = is_resume_file(basename(raw_file_list)),
  modified_time = file.info(raw_file_list)$mtime
) %>%
  filter(!is.na(pid), !is.na(wave)) %>%
  arrange(pid, wave, modified_time)

cat("Raw files found:", length(raw_file_list), "\n")
cat("Files with valid PID + wave:", nrow(file_key), "\n")

combine_index <- file_key %>%
  group_by(pid, wave) %>%
  summarise(
    files = list(file),
    source_filenames = paste(filename, collapse = " | "),
    n_files_for_pid_wave = n(),
    has_resume_file = any(is_resume),
    was_combined = n() > 1 & any(is_resume),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

file_list <- c()
combine_audit_rows <- list()

for (i in seq_len(nrow(combine_index))) {
  
  pid_i   <- combine_index$pid[i]
  wave_i  <- combine_index$wave[i]
  files_i <- combine_index$files[[i]]
  was_combined_i <- combine_index$was_combined[i]
  
  if (was_combined_i) {
    
    combined_data <- map_dfr(seq_along(files_i), function(j) {
      read.csv(files_i[j], stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
        mutate(
          source_file = basename(files_i[j]),
          source_file_order = j,
          was_combined = TRUE
        )
    })
    
    out_name <- sprintf("Chein-LITe.%s_P%04d_CRASH_RESUME_COMBINED.csv", wave_i, pid_i)
    out_path <- file.path(combined_dir, out_name)
    
    write.csv(combined_data, out_path, row.names = FALSE)
    
    file_list <- c(file_list, out_path)
    
    message(
      "COMBINED crash/resume files | PID ", pid_i,
      " | wave ", wave_i,
      " | files combined: ", length(files_i),
      " -> ", out_name
    )
    
  } else {
    
    # No true crash/resume combination.
    # Use the original raw file as-is.
    file_list <- c(file_list, files_i[1])
    
    message(
      "NOT combined | PID ", pid_i,
      " | wave ", wave_i,
      " | using original file: ", basename(files_i[1])
    )
  }
  
  combine_audit_rows[[i]] <- data.frame(
    pid = pid_i,
    wave = wave_i,
    n_files_for_pid_wave = combine_index$n_files_for_pid_wave[i],
    has_resume_file = combine_index$has_resume_file[i],
    was_combined = was_combined_i,
    source_filenames = combine_index$source_filenames[i],
    file_used_downstream = basename(file_list[length(file_list)])
  )
}

combine_audit <- bind_rows(combine_audit_rows)

combine_audit_output <- file.path(root_dir, "combined_pid_wave_audit.csv")
write_csv(combine_audit, combine_audit_output)

cat("\nFiles sent downstream:", length(file_list), "\n")
cat("Actually combined crash/resume cases:", sum(combine_audit$was_combined), "\n")
cat("Combine audit saved to:", combine_audit_output, "\n\n")
# ============================================================
# HELPER FUNCTIONS
# ============================================================

# Extract participant ID from filename
# Handles optional P/p prefix, 3-4 digit IDs, followed by a year
#extract_pid <- function(filename) {
 #pid <- str_match(filename, "(?i)(?:P)?(\\d{3,4})(?=[-_\\.](?:19|20)\\d{2})")
  #as.numeric(pid[, 2])
#}

# Parse RT column: "[]" or empty or "None" -> NA, otherwise numeric
parse_rt <- function(x) {
  x <- as.character(x)
  x[x == "[]" | x == "" | x == "None" | is.na(x)] <- NA
  as.numeric(x)
}

# Alias for parse_rt — used for non-RT timing columns
parse_num <- parse_rt

# D-prime with loglinear correction (avoids 0/1 rates)
compute_dprime <- function(n_hit, n_miss, n_fa, n_cr) {
  hit_rate <- (n_hit + 0.5) / (n_hit + n_miss + 1)
  fa_rate  <- (n_fa  + 0.5) / (n_fa  + n_cr  + 1)
  qnorm(hit_rate) - qnorm(fa_rate)
}

# Build BIDS trial_type label from condition, target type, accuracy, and late response.
# Late responses (lateResp.keys present) are recoded to "Late_Response" regardless
# of trial type or accuracy, pulling them out of regressors of interest into a
# dedicated nuisance EV. All other trials follow the standard hit/miss/CR/FA scheme.
make_trial_type <- function(condition, target_type, accuracy, late_keys) {
  cond    <- ifelse(is.na(condition), "Unknown",
                    ifelse(condition == "low", "Low", "High"))
  is_late <- !is.na(late_keys) & late_keys != "None" & late_keys != ""
  outcome <- ifelse(is_late, "Late_Response",
             ifelse(target_type == "go"   & accuracy == 1, "Hit",
             ifelse(target_type == "go"   & accuracy == 0, "Miss",
             ifelse(target_type == "nogo" & accuracy == 1, "CR",
             ifelse(target_type == "nogo" & accuracy == 0, "FA", "Unknown")))))
  ifelse(outcome == "Late_Response", "Late_Response",
         paste0(cond, "_", ifelse(target_type == "go", "Go", "NoGo"), "_", outcome))
}

# ============================================================
# SECTION 1: FILE AUDIT
# Checks all CSVs for required trial structure and file version.
# Files using the older task version (no waitForScanner.stopped)
# are flagged and excluded from all downstream processing.
# ============================================================

#file_list <- list.files(path = root_dir, pattern = "\\.csv$",
                       # recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)

audit <- data.frame()

for (file in file_list) {
  data         <- read.csv(file, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

  file_version <- ifelse("waitForScanner.stopped" %in% colnames(data), "new", "old")
  valid_trials <- data[!is.na(data$thisTargetType) & data$thisTargetType != "", ]

  n_trials <- nrow(valid_trials)
  n_late   <- if ("lateResp.keys" %in% colnames(data)) {
    sum(!is.na(valid_trials$lateResp.keys) &
        valid_trials$lateResp.keys != "" &
        valid_trials$lateResp.keys != "None", na.rm = TRUE)
  } else { NA_integer_ }
  pct_late <- ifelse(n_trials > 0 & !is.na(n_late),
                     round(100 * n_late / n_trials, 1), NA_real_)

  audit <- rbind(audit, data.frame(
    filename     = basename(file),
    pid          = extract_pid(basename(file)),
    file_version = file_version,
    total_rows   = nrow(data),
    n_trials     = n_trials,
    n_go         = sum(valid_trials$thisTargetType == "go",   na.rm = TRUE),
    n_nogo       = sum(valid_trials$thisTargetType == "nogo", na.rm = TRUE),
    n_low        = sum(valid_trials$valueCondition == "low",  na.rm = TRUE),
    n_high       = sum(valid_trials$valueCondition == "high", na.rm = TRUE),
    has_RT_col   = "RT" %in% colnames(data),
    n_late       = n_late,
    pct_late     = pct_late,
    flag         = file_version == "old" |
                   n_trials == 0 |
                   sum(valid_trials$thisTargetType == "go",   na.rm = TRUE) == 0 |
                   sum(valid_trials$thisTargetType == "nogo", na.rm = TRUE) == 0
  ))
}

cat("Total files:", nrow(audit), "\n")
cat("New-version files:", sum(audit$file_version == "new"), "\n")
cat("Old-version files excluded:", sum(audit$file_version == "old"), "\n")
cat("Files flagged as problematic (other):", sum(audit$flag & audit$file_version == "new"), "\n\n")

if (any(audit$file_version == "old")) {
  cat("--- Excluded (old version, no waitForScanner.stopped) ---\n")
  print(audit[audit$file_version == "old", c("filename", "pid", "file_version", "n_trials")])
  cat("\n")
}

if (any(audit$flag & audit$file_version == "new")) {
  cat("--- Flagged (new version but bad data) ---\n")
  print(audit[audit$flag & audit$file_version == "new", ])
  cat("\n")
}

# Remove all flagged files (old version + bad data) from processing list
bad_files <- audit$filename[audit$flag]
file_list <- file_list[!basename(file_list) %in% bad_files]
cat("Files remaining after audit:", length(file_list), "\n\n")

# ============================================================
# SECTION 2: BEHAVIORAL METRICS EXTRACTION
# Computes accuracy, RT, and d-prime overall and by stake condition
# ============================================================

results <- data.frame()

for (file in file_list) {
  data           <- read.csv(file, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  data$RT_num    <- parse_rt(data$RT)
  data$goRT_num  <- parse_rt(data$goRT)
  data           <- data[!is.na(data$thisTargetType) & data$thisTargetType != "", ]

  # --- Helper: compute metrics for a trial subset ---
  compute_metrics <- function(go, nogo) {
    n_hit  <- sum(go$Accuracy   == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
    n_miss <- sum(go$Accuracy   == 0, na.rm = TRUE)
    n_cr   <- sum(nogo$Accuracy == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
    n_fa   <- sum(nogo$Accuracy == 0, na.rm = TRUE)
    list(
      gacc   = n_hit / (n_hit + n_miss),
      ngacc  = n_cr  / (n_cr  + n_fa),
      dprime = compute_dprime(n_hit, n_miss, n_fa, n_cr),
      goRT   = mean(go$RT_num[go$Accuracy == 1],     na.rm = TRUE),
      goRTsd = sd(go$RT_num[go$Accuracy == 1],       na.rm = TRUE),
      nogoRT = mean(nogo$RT_num[nogo$Accuracy == 0], na.rm = TRUE),
      hits = n_hit, misses = n_miss, CRs = n_cr, FAs = n_fa
    )
  }

  overall <- compute_metrics(
    data[data$thisTargetType == "go", ],
    data[data$thisTargetType == "nogo", ]
  )
  low <- compute_metrics(
    data[data$thisTargetType == "go"   & data$valueCondition == "low", ],
    data[data$thisTargetType == "nogo" & data$valueCondition == "low", ]
  )
  high <- compute_metrics(
    data[data$thisTargetType == "go"   & data$valueCondition == "high", ],
    data[data$thisTargetType == "nogo" & data$valueCondition == "high", ]
  )

  results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
    filename       = basename(file),
    pid_assignment = extract_pid(basename(file)),
    GoAcc          = overall$gacc,   GoRT       = overall$goRT,
    NoGoAcc        = overall$ngacc,  NoGoRT     = overall$nogoRT,
    LowGoAcc       = low$gacc,       LowGoRT    = low$goRT,   LowRTVar   = low$goRTsd,
    HighGoAcc      = high$gacc,      HighGoRT   = high$goRT,  HighRTVar  = high$goRTsd,
    LowNoGoAcc     = low$ngacc,      LowNoGoRT  = low$nogoRT,
    HighNoGoAcc    = high$ngacc,     HighNoGoRT = high$nogoRT,
    GoAccDiff      = high$gacc - low$gacc,
    Dprime         = overall$dprime,
    LowDprime      = low$dprime,
    HighDprime     = high$dprime,
    hits           = overall$hits,   misses     = overall$misses,
    CRs            = overall$CRs,    FAs        = overall$FAs
  ))
}

# Merge SubjectAge from external source if available:
# results <- merge(results, redcap_data[, c("pid_assignment", "SubjectAge")],
#                  by = "pid_assignment", all.x = TRUE)

write.csv(results, file = beh_output, row.names = FALSE)
cat("Behavioral results saved to:", beh_output, "\n")

# --- Behavioral summary table: group means (SD) overall and by stake condition ---
fmt <- function(x) sprintf("%.3f (%.3f)", mean(x, na.rm = TRUE), sd(x, na.rm = TRUE))

summary_table <- data.frame(
  Metric = c(
    "Go Accuracy",     "NoGo Accuracy",    "Go RT (ms)",      "NoGo FA RT (ms)",
    "Go RT SD (ms)",   "D-prime",
    "Low Go Accuracy", "High Go Accuracy",
    "Low NoGo Accuracy","High NoGo Accuracy",
    "Low Go RT (ms)",  "High Go RT (ms)",
    "Low Go RT SD",    "High Go RT SD",
    "Low D-prime",     "High D-prime",
    "Go Acc Diff (High - Low)"
  ),
  Mean_SD = c(
    fmt(results$GoAcc),      fmt(results$NoGoAcc),    fmt(results$GoRT),       fmt(results$NoGoRT),
    fmt(results$LowRTVar),   fmt(results$Dprime),
    fmt(results$LowGoAcc),   fmt(results$HighGoAcc),
    fmt(results$LowNoGoAcc), fmt(results$HighNoGoAcc),
    fmt(results$LowGoRT),    fmt(results$HighGoRT),
    fmt(results$LowRTVar),   fmt(results$HighRTVar),
    fmt(results$LowDprime),  fmt(results$HighDprime),
    fmt(results$GoAccDiff)
  )
)

cat("\n=== Behavioral Summary (N =", nrow(results), ") — Mean (SD) ===\n")
cat(sprintf("%-30s %s\n", "Metric", "Mean (SD)"))
cat(strrep("-", 52), "\n")
cat(sprintf("%-30s %s\n\n", "--- Overall ---", ""))
for (i in 1:6)  cat(sprintf("  %-28s %s\n", summary_table$Metric[i],  summary_table$Mean_SD[i]))
cat(sprintf("\n%-30s %s\n", "--- By Stake Condition ---", ""))
for (i in 7:17) cat(sprintf("  %-28s %s\n", summary_table$Metric[i],  summary_table$Mean_SD[i]))
cat("\n")

# ============================================================
# SECTION 3: BIDS EVENTS.TSV GENERATION
# One TSV per subject per run with full trial metadata
# t=0 anchored to waitForScanner.stopped per run
# ============================================================

dir.create(events_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)
generated <- c()

for (file in file_list) {
  data           <- read.csv(file, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  pid            <- extract_pid(basename(file))
  sub_label      <- sprintf("sub-%04d", pid)
  trials         <- data[!is.na(data$thisTargetType) & data$thisTargetType != "", ]

  if (nrow(trials) == 0) {
    message("Skipping (no valid trials): ", basename(file))
    next
  }

  trials$RT_num <- parse_rt(trials$RT)
  runs <- unique(trials$Run)
  runs <- runs[!is.na(runs) & runs != ""]

  for (run in runs) {
    run_trials <- trials[trials$Run == run, ]
    run_data   <- data[!is.na(data$Run) & data$Run == run, ]

    # t=0 anchor: first scanner trigger for this run
    wfs_vals <- as.numeric(run_data$waitForScanner.stopped)
    wfs_vals <- wfs_vals[!is.na(wfs_vals)]
    run_t0   <- wfs_vals[1]

    if (is.na(run_t0)) {
      message("WARNING: No waitForScanner.stopped for ", run, " in ", basename(file))
      next
    }

    # Cue events (one per block, deduplicated by onset)
    cue_rows <- run_trials[!duplicated(run_trials$cueOnset), ]
    cue_rows <- cue_rows[!is.na(cue_rows$cueOnset) & cue_rows$cueOnset != "", ]

    # ----------------------------------------------------------
    # CUE EVENTS (one per block)
    # ----------------------------------------------------------
    cue_events <- data.frame(
      onset             = as.numeric(cue_rows$cueOnset)  - run_t0,
      duration          = as.numeric(cue_rows$cueOffset) - as.numeric(cue_rows$cueOnset),
      trial_type        = paste0(ifelse(cue_rows$valueCondition == "low", "Low", "High"), "_Cue"),
      valueCondition    = cue_rows$valueCondition,
      thisTargetType    = NA,
      accuracy          = NA,
      RT                = NA,
      fixation_onset    = NA,
      fixation_duration = NA,
      isi_onset         = NA,
      isi_duration      = NA,
      go_prepotency     = NA,
      nogo_prepotency   = NA
    )

    # ----------------------------------------------------------
    # TRIAL EVENTS (target onset)
    # ----------------------------------------------------------

    # fixation onset & duration
    fix_started <- parse_num(run_trials$fixation.started)
    fix_stopped <- parse_num(run_trials$fixation.stopped)

    # jittered ISI onset & duration
    isi_started <- parse_num(run_trials$jitteredISI.started)
    isi_stopped <- parse_num(run_trials$jitteredISI.stopped)

    trial_events <- data.frame(
      onset             = as.numeric(run_trials$targetOnset) - run_t0,
      duration          = as.numeric(run_trials$trial.stopped) - as.numeric(run_trials$trial.started),
      trial_type        = make_trial_type(run_trials$valueCondition,
                                          run_trials$thisTargetType,
                                          run_trials$Accuracy,
                                          run_trials$lateResp.keys),
      valueCondition    = run_trials$valueCondition,
      thisTargetType    = run_trials$thisTargetType,
      accuracy          = run_trials$Accuracy,
      RT                = run_trials$RT_num,
      fixation_onset    = ifelse(is.na(fix_started), NA, fix_started - run_t0),
      fixation_duration = ifelse(is.na(fix_started) | is.na(fix_stopped), NA,
                                 fix_stopped - fix_started),
      isi_onset         = ifelse(is.na(isi_started), NA, isi_started - run_t0),
      isi_duration      = ifelse(is.na(isi_started) | is.na(isi_stopped), NA,
                                 isi_stopped - isi_started),
      go_prepotency     = parse_num(run_trials$thisTrialGoPrepot),
      nogo_prepotency   = parse_num(run_trials$thisTrialNoGoPrepot)
    )

    # ----------------------------------------------------------
    # COMBINE, SORT, ROUND
    # reward.started is numeric; !is.na filter sufficient — no "" comparison needed
    # ----------------------------------------------------------
    rew_onsets  <- run_data$reward.started[!is.na(run_data$reward.started)] - run_t0
    reward_cues <- if (length(rew_onsets) > 0) {
      data.frame(onset = rew_onsets, duration = 1.5, trial_type = "Reward",
                 valueCondition = NA, thisTargetType = NA, accuracy = NA,
                 RT = NA, fixation_onset = NA, fixation_duration = NA,
                 isi_onset = NA, isi_duration = NA,
                 go_prepotency = NA, nogo_prepotency = NA)
    } else {
      message("WARNING: No reward events for ", run, " in ", basename(file))
      NULL
    }
    events <- rbind(cue_events, trial_events, reward_cues)
    events           <- events[order(events$onset), ]
    rownames(events) <- NULL
    events$onset             <- round(events$onset,             4)
    events$duration          <- round(events$duration,          4)
    events$fixation_onset    <- round(events$fixation_onset,    4)
    events$fixation_duration <- round(events$fixation_duration, 4)
    events$isi_onset         <- round(events$isi_onset,         4)
    events$isi_duration      <- round(events$isi_duration,      4)

    run_num   <- as.integer(gsub("Run", "", run))
    out_fname <- sprintf("%s_task-gonogo_run-%d_events.tsv", sub_label, run_num)
    out_path  <- file.path(events_dir, out_fname)

    write.table(events, out_path, sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE,
                quote = FALSE, na = "n/a")

    generated <- c(generated, out_fname)
    message("Written: ", out_fname)
  }
}

cat("\nTotal events files generated:", length(generated), "\n")
cat("Unique participants:", length(unique(gsub("_task-gonogo.*", "", generated))), "\n")
run_counts <- table(gsub("_task-gonogo.*", "", generated))
cat("Participants with all 4 runs:", sum(run_counts == 4), "\n")
cat("Participants with fewer than 4 runs:", sum(run_counts < 4), "\n")
print(run_counts[run_counts < 4])
# ============================================================
# SECTION 4: FSL REGRESSOR FILES  [REVISED — collapsed EVs]
# 3-column space-separated: onset duration amplitude
# One .txt file per EV per run per subject
#
# Regressors (10 EVs):
#   hit           — correct go (High + Low collapsed)
#   miss          — missed go (High + Low collapsed)
#   CR            — correct nogo / successful inhibition (High + Low collapsed)
#   FA            — false alarm / failed inhibition (High + Low collapsed)
#   low_cue       — low-stake block cue onset (nuisance)
#   high_cue      — high-stake block cue onset (nuisance)
#   reward        — reward feedback onset (nuisance)
#   late_response — responses after 500ms target window (nuisance)
# ============================================================

tsv_files <- list.files(events_dir, pattern = "\\.tsv$", full.names = TRUE)

# Patterns matched against trial_type column in the TSV.
# Go_Hit$, Go_Miss$, etc. match both Low_ and High_ prefixes by anchoring on suffix.
# Cue EVs use exact match to preserve low/high separation.
regressors <- c(
  "hit"           = "Go_Hit$",
  "miss"          = "Go_Miss$",
  "CR"            = "NoGo_CR$",
  "FA"            = "NoGo_FA$",
  "low_cue"       = "^Low_Cue$",
  "high_cue"      = "^High_Cue$",
  "reward"        = "^Reward$",
  "late_response" = "^Late_Response$"
)

for (f in tsv_files) {
  d    <- read_tsv(f, na = "n/a", show_col_types = FALSE)
  base <- str_remove(basename(f), "\\.tsv$")

  for (reg_name in names(regressors)) {
    pattern <- regressors[[reg_name]]

    reg_df <- d %>%
      filter(str_detect(trial_type, pattern)) %>%
      select(onset, duration) %>%
      mutate(amplitude = 1) %>%
      arrange(onset)

    if (nrow(reg_df) == 0) {
      message("WARNING: No trials for ", reg_name, " in ", basename(f))
      next
    }

    out_path <- file.path(output_dir, sprintf("%s_%s.txt", base, reg_name))
    write_delim(reg_df, out_path, delim = " ", col_names = FALSE, na = "n/a")
    message("Written: ", basename(out_path))
  }
}

# ============================================================
# SECTION 5: TRIAL COUNT AUDIT  [REVISED — collapsed EVs]
# Flags sparse/empty cells per regressor per run
# Use output CSV to QC before running FSL
# ============================================================

# Audit only the regressors of interest (not nuisance EVs)
audit_regressors <- c(
  "hit"  = "Go_Hit$",
  "miss" = "Go_Miss$",
  "CR"   = "NoGo_CR$",
  "FA"   = "NoGo_FA$"
)

trial_counts <- map_dfr(tsv_files, function(f) {
  d   <- read_tsv(f, na = "n/a", show_col_types = FALSE)
  sub <- str_extract(basename(f), "sub-\\d+")
  run <- str_extract(basename(f), "run-\\d+")
  map_dfr(names(audit_regressors), function(reg_name) {
    data.frame(
      subject   = sub,
      run       = run,
      regressor = reg_name,
      n_trials  = sum(str_detect(d$trial_type, audit_regressors[[reg_name]]),
                      na.rm = TRUE)
    )
  })
}) %>%
  mutate(flag = case_when(
    n_trials == 0               ~ "EMPTY",
    n_trials < sparse_threshold ~ "SPARSE",
    TRUE                        ~ "ok"
  ))

cat("\n=== Trial count summary per regressor (collapsed) ===\n")
trial_counts %>%
  group_by(regressor) %>%
  summarise(mean = round(mean(n_trials), 1), min = min(n_trials),
            max = max(n_trials), n_empty = sum(flag == "EMPTY"),
            n_sparse = sum(flag == "SPARSE"), .groups = "drop") %>%
  print(n = Inf)

write_csv(trial_counts, audit_output)
cat("Trial count audit saved to:", audit_output, "\n")


# ============================================================
# SECTION 4: FSL REGRESSOR FILES  [REVISED — collapsed EVs]
# 3-column space-separated: onset duration amplitude
# One .txt file per EV per run per subject
#
# Regressors (10 EVs):
#   hit           — correct go (High + Low collapsed)
#   miss          — missed go (High + Low collapsed)
#   CR            — correct nogo / successful inhibition (High + Low collapsed)
#   FA            — false alarm / failed inhibition (High + Low collapsed)
#   low_cue       — low-stake block cue onset (nuisance)
#   high_cue      — high-stake block cue onset (nuisance)
#   reward        — reward feedback onset (nuisance)
#   late_response — responses after 500ms target window (nuisance)
# ============================================================

tsv_files <- list.files(events_dir, pattern = "\\.tsv$", full.names = TRUE)

# Patterns matched against trial_type column in the TSV.
# Go_Hit$, Go_Miss$, etc. match both Low_ and High_ prefixes by anchoring on suffix.
# Cue EVs use exact match to preserve low/high separation.
regressors <- c(
  "hit"           = "Go_Hit$",
  "miss"          = "Go_Miss$",
  "CR"            = "NoGo_CR$",
  "FA"            = "NoGo_FA$",
  "low_cue"       = "^Low_Cue$",
  "high_cue"      = "^High_Cue$",
  "reward"        = "^Reward$",
  "late_response" = "^Late_Response$"
)

for (f in tsv_files) {
  d    <- read_tsv(f, na = "n/a", show_col_types = FALSE)
  base <- str_remove(basename(f), "\\.tsv$")

  for (reg_name in names(regressors)) {
    pattern <- regressors[[reg_name]]

    reg_df <- d %>%
      filter(str_detect(trial_type, pattern)) %>%
      select(onset, duration) %>%
      mutate(amplitude = 1) %>%
      arrange(onset)

    if (nrow(reg_df) == 0) {
      message("WARNING: No trials for ", reg_name, " in ", basename(f))
      next
    }

    out_path <- file.path(output_dir, sprintf("%s_%s.txt", base, reg_name))
    write_delim(reg_df, out_path, delim = " ", col_names = FALSE, na = "n/a")
    message("Written: ", basename(out_path))
  }
}

# ============================================================
# SECTION 5: TRIAL COUNT AUDIT  [REVISED — collapsed EVs + EV presence table]
# Flags sparse/empty cells per regressor per run
# Use output CSV to QC before running FSL
# ============================================================

audit_regressors <- c(
  "hit"  = "Go_Hit$",
  "miss" = "Go_Miss$",
  "CR"   = "NoGo_CR$",
  "FA"   = "NoGo_FA$"
)

trial_counts <- map_dfr(tsv_files, function(f) {
  d   <- read_tsv(f, na = "n/a", show_col_types = FALSE)
  sub <- str_extract(basename(f), "sub-\\d+")
  run <- str_extract(basename(f), "run-\\d+")
  map_dfr(names(audit_regressors), function(reg_name) {
    data.frame(
      subject   = sub,
      run       = run,
      regressor = reg_name,
      n_trials  = sum(str_detect(d$trial_type, audit_regressors[[reg_name]]),
                      na.rm = TRUE)
    )
  })
}) %>%
  mutate(flag = case_when(
    n_trials == 0               ~ "EMPTY",
    n_trials < sparse_threshold ~ "SPARSE",
    TRUE                        ~ "ok"
  ))

cat("\n=== Trial count summary per regressor (collapsed) ===\n")
trial_counts %>%
  group_by(regressor) %>%
  summarise(mean = round(mean(n_trials), 1), min = min(n_trials),
            max = max(n_trials), n_empty = sum(flag == "EMPTY"),
            n_sparse = sum(flag == "SPARSE"), .groups = "drop") %>%
  print(n = Inf)

write_csv(trial_counts, audit_output)
cat("Trial count audit saved to:", audit_output, "\n")

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# EV PRESENCE TABLE
# Wide-format table: one row per subject per run, one column
# per regressor of interest. Values are flag labels (ok /
# SPARSE / EMPTY) so you can see at a glance which run-level
# FEAT models need a reduced EV set.
# ----------------------------------------------------------

ev_presence <- trial_counts %>%
  select(subject, run, regressor, flag) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = regressor, values_from = flag) %>%
  arrange(subject, run)

cat("\n=== EV presence table (ok / SPARSE / EMPTY) ===\n")
print(ev_presence, n = Inf)

# Summary: how many subjects have at least one problematic run per EV
cat("\n=== Subjects with at least one EMPTY run per EV ===\n")
ev_presence %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(names(audit_regressors)),
                   ~ sum(. == "EMPTY", na.rm = TRUE),
                   .names = "{.col}_n_empty_runs")) %>%
  print()

cat("\n=== Subjects with at least one SPARSE run per EV ===\n")
ev_presence %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(names(audit_regressors)),
                   ~ sum(. == "SPARSE", na.rm = TRUE),
                   .names = "{.col}_n_sparse_runs")) %>%
  print()

# Save EV presence table to its own CSV for reference when building FEAT models
ev_presence_output <- file.path(dirname(audit_output), "ev_presence_table.csv")
write_csv(ev_presence, ev_presence_output)
cat("\nEV presence table saved to:", ev_presence_output, "\n")

# ============================================================
# FSL PILOT SELECTION (EV-aware)
# Selects 30 clean participants balanced across PID groups:
#   10 from 1000s, 10 from 2000s, 10 from 3000s
# Criteria per participant:
#   1. Not flagged (passes structural audit)
#   2. Late response rate by age group (children 20%, adolescents/adults 10%)
#   3. All 4 runs present within the CSV
#   4. No EMPTY cells for any regressor of interest across runs
#   5. Within each group, sorted by PID for reproducibility
# Participants with only SPARSE (no EMPTY) cells are included
# but flagged in the output table.
# Note: Final selection and printing occurs at end of Section 5
# after ev_presence table is built.
# ============================================================

n_per_group <- 15

# Criterion 1: PIDs passing structural audit
clean_pids <- audit$pid[!audit$flag & !is.na(audit$pid)]

# Criterion 2: PIDs with late response rate <= threshold by age group
low_late_pids <- audit$pid[!is.na(audit$pct_late) & (
  (audit$pid >= 1000 & audit$pid < 2000 & audit$pct_late <= (late_resp_threshold_children    * 100)) |
    (audit$pid >= 2000 & audit$pid < 3000 & audit$pct_late <= (late_resp_threshold_adolescents * 100)) |
    (audit$pid >= 3000 & audit$pid < 4000 & audit$pct_late <= (late_resp_threshold_adults      * 100))
)]

# Criterion 3: PIDs with all 4 runs present
all_4_runs_pids <- sapply(file_list, function(f) {
  d    <- read.csv(f, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  runs <- unique(d$Run[!is.na(d$Run) & d$Run != ""])
  length(runs) == 4
})
all_4_runs_pids <- extract_pid(basename(file_list[all_4_runs_pids]))

# Criteria 1-3 combined (criterion 4 applied in Section 5 after ev_presence is built)
structurally_clean <- audit %>%
  filter(!flag, !is.na(pct_late), pid %in% low_late_pids) %>%
  mutate(subject = sprintf("sub-%04d", pid)) %>%
  pull(subject)

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# FSL PILOT SELECTION — final selection (EV-aware, criterion 4)
# Depends on ev_presence and audit_regressors from Section 5
# ----------------------------------------------------------

# Criterion 4: subjects with at least one EMPTY cell in any EV — exclude
subjects_with_empty <- ev_presence %>%
  filter(if_any(all_of(names(audit_regressors)), ~ . == "EMPTY")) %>%
  pull(subject) %>%
  unique()

# Subjects with at least one SPARSE cell — include but flag
subjects_with_sparse <- ev_presence %>%
  filter(if_any(all_of(names(audit_regressors)), ~ . == "SPARSE")) %>%
  pull(subject) %>%
  unique()

# Apply all criteria
ev_eligible <- ev_presence %>%
  select(subject) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  filter(
    !subject %in% subjects_with_empty,                        # criterion 4: no empty EVs
    subject %in% structurally_clean,                          # criteria 1 & 2: structural audit + late response rate
    subject %in% sprintf("sub-%04d", all_4_runs_pids)         # criterion 3: all 4 runs present
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pid        = as.integer(str_extract(subject, "\\d+")),
    has_sparse = subject %in% subjects_with_sparse
  ) %>%
  arrange(pid)                                                 # criterion 5: sorted by PID

# Split into PID groups and select top 10 per group
fsl_group_1000 <- ev_eligible %>% filter(pid >= 1000, pid < 2000)
fsl_group_2000 <- ev_eligible %>% filter(pid >= 2000, pid < 3000)
fsl_group_3000 <- ev_eligible %>% filter(pid >= 3000, pid < 4000)

select_fsl_group <- function(df, n, label) {
  if (nrow(df) >= n) {
    df[1:n, ]
  } else {
    message(sprintf("WARNING: Only %d eligible in %s group (wanted %d)",
                    nrow(df), label, n))
    df
  }
}

fsl_pilot <- bind_rows(
  select_fsl_group(fsl_group_1000, n_per_group, "1000s") %>% mutate(group = "1000s"),
  select_fsl_group(fsl_group_2000, n_per_group, "2000s") %>% mutate(group = "2000s"),
  select_fsl_group(fsl_group_3000, n_per_group, "3000s") %>% mutate(group = "3000s")
)

# Join pct_late from audit for display
fsl_pilot <- fsl_pilot %>%
  left_join(
    audit %>%
      mutate(subject = sprintf("sub-%04d", pid)) %>%
      select(subject, pct_late),
    by = "subject"
  )

cat("\n=== FSL Pilot Selection — EV-aware (N =", nrow(fsl_pilot), ") ===\n")
cat(sprintf("  Excluded for EMPTY EVs:    %d\n", length(subjects_with_empty)))
cat(sprintf("  Eligible 1000s: %d  |  Selected: %d\n",
            nrow(fsl_group_1000), sum(fsl_pilot$group == "1000s")))
cat(sprintf("  Eligible 2000s: %d  |  Selected: %d\n",
            nrow(fsl_group_2000), sum(fsl_pilot$group == "2000s")))
cat(sprintf("  Eligible 3000s: %d  |  Selected: %d\n",
            nrow(fsl_group_3000), sum(fsl_pilot$group == "3000s")))
cat("\n")
print(fsl_pilot %>% select(group, subject, pid, has_sparse, pct_late),
      row.names = FALSE)
#ANDREWWWWWW _ DOnt think this is needed for cleaning but i am young and naive so it may be 
#fsl_pilot <- fsl_pilot %>%
  #left_join(
   # demo_raw %>%
     # mutate(subject = sprintf("sub-%04d", pid_assignment)) %>%
     # select(subject, age = participant_age),
   # by = "subject"
 # )

# Save pilot list
fsl_pilot_output <- file.path(dirname(audit_output), "fsl_pilot_subjects.csv")
write_csv(fsl_pilot, fsl_pilot_output)
cat("\nFSL pilot subject list saved to:", fsl_pilot_output, "\n")

```
